cards.title: Sleep Hygiene Protocol
cards._legacyHtml:
CBT-I core components:
1. Sleep restriction: Limit time in bed to actual sleep time. If sleeping 5 hrs, set bed time window to 5 hrs. Extend by 15 min weekly when efficiency >85%.
2. Stimulus control: Bed only for sleep and sex. If awake >20 min, get up. No screens in bedroom.
3. Sleep hygiene rules: Fixed wake time daily (even weekends). No naps >20 min. No caffeine after 12 PM. Dim lights 2 hrs before bed. Room temp 18–20°C. No alcohol within 3 hrs of bed.
4. Relaxation: Progressive muscle relaxation or 4-7-8 breathing at bedtime.
5. Cognitive restructuring: Address sleep anxiety. Use sleep diary.
Supplements (naturopathic): Ashwagandha 300 mg (KSM-66), Magnesium glycinate 400 mg, L-theanine 200 mg, Melatonin 0.5–1 mg (short-term only).
Evidence: CBT-I: Level 1A. First-line for chronic insomnia.

cards.title: Stress Management — Clinical Tools
cards._legacyHtml:
HRV Biofeedback: Train 0.1 Hz resonance breathing (5–6 breaths/min). Increases HRV and vagal tone. Apps: Elite HRV, HeartMath. Practice 20 min/day × 6 weeks. Evidence: Level 2A for anxiety, hypertension.
Mindfulness-Based Stress Reduction (MBSR): 8-week structured programme. 45 min/day formal practice. Body scan, sitting meditation, mindful movement. Evidence: Level 1A for anxiety and chronic pain.
Physiological sigh: Double inhale (nose) + long slow exhale (mouth). One breath reduces acute stress. Fastest known physiological stress down-regulator (Balban et al., Cell Reports Medicine 2023).
Journaling: Expressive writing 15 min × 3 days. Reduces cortisol, improves immune function. Gratitude journaling (3 items nightly) ↑ wellbeing scores.
Key labs for stress assessment: Morning cortisol (salivary 4-point), DHEA-S, HRV (resting), CBC for lymphocyte count.

cards.title: Exercise Prescription — FITT Principle
cards._legacyHtml:
FITT Framework:
F — Frequency: Aerobic: 3–5 days/week. Resistance: 2–3 days/week (non-consecutive).
I — Intensity: Moderate: 50–70% HRmax (HRmax = 220 − age). RPE 12–14 (Borg scale). Can hold a conversation.
T — Time: 150 min/week moderate OR 75 min/week vigorous aerobic. Resistance: 2–3 sets × 8–12 reps per muscle group.
T — Type: Aerobic: walking, cycling, swimming, dancing. Resistance: bodyweight, weights, resistance bands.
Condition-specific:
• T2DM: Post-meal 15-min walk (↓ glucose spike by 30%). Resistance training ↑ insulin sensitivity.
• Hypertension: Moderate aerobic ↓ SBP 5–8 mmHg. Isometric handgrip (4×2 min) ↓ SBP 10 mmHg.
• Depression: 30 min aerobic 3x/week = antidepressant effect (Level 1A).
• Osteoporosis: Weight-bearing + resistance training. Avoid high-impact if fracture risk.

cards.title: Smoking Cessation Protocol
cards._legacyHtml:
Stages of change model: Precontemplation → Contemplation → Preparation → Action → Maintenance. Tailor intervention to stage.
5 A's Framework: Ask (tobacco use), Advise (quit), Assess (readiness), Assist (cessation plan), Arrange (follow-up in 1 week).
Quit date: Set specific date within 2 weeks. Remove cigarettes/lighters/ashtrays on quit day.
NRT options: Patch (21→14→7 mg over 8–12 weeks), gum (2 mg or 4 mg PRN), lozenge, inhaler. Combine patch + PRN form for best results.
Naturopathic support:
• Lobelia inflata (1:3 tincture, 1–2 ml TDS — lobeline reduces craving)
• Ashwagandha (stress management)
• Oats (Avena sativa) — reduces nicotine craving
• Acupuncture (Lung 7, Shen Men ear point)
Behavioural: Identify triggers, delay-distract-decide technique, deep breathing for cravings (lasts 3–5 min). 4D technique: Delay, Deep breathe, Drink water, Do something else.

cards.title: Digital Detox & Screen Hygiene
cards._legacyHtml:
Blue light & melatonin: Screens emit blue light (380–500 nm) → suppresses melatonin by 50% if used within 2 hours of bed. Use blue-light filter after sunset or blue-light-blocking glasses.
20-20-20 rule: Every 20 minutes of screen work → look at something 20 feet away for 20 seconds → reduces digital eye strain.
Screen time guidelines: Adults: no screens 1 hour before bed. Children <2 yrs: no screens. Children 2–5 yrs: <1 hr/day. Children 6+: consistent limits. Adolescents: ≤2 hr recreational screens/day.
Digital detox prescription: Phone-free meals. Phone charges outside bedroom. Social media time-box (30 min/day set times). Notification audit — turn off all non-essential. One full day/month screen-free.
Dopamine fasting: 24-hour abstinence from all digital stimulation (social media, entertainment, news). Evidence emerging for anxiety reduction and attention restoration.

cards.title: Lifestyle Pillars — Prescriptive Summary
cards._legacyHtml:
6 Pillars of Lifestyle Medicine (ACLM):
1. Nutrition: Whole food plant-predominant diet. Mediterranean or anti-inflammatory pattern.
2. Physical Activity: 150 min/week moderate aerobic + 2x resistance training.
3. Restorative Sleep: 7–9 hours. Fixed wake time. CBT-I for insomnia.
4. Stress Management: MBSR, breathwork, HRV biofeedback, nature exposure.
5. Avoidance of Risky Substances: No tobacco. Alcohol <1 drink/day (women), <2 (men) — naturopathic ideal: none.
6. Positive Social Connection: Loneliness ↑ mortality equivalent to smoking 15 cigarettes/day. Prescribe social engagement, community, purpose.
BNYS additions: Dinacharya (daily routine), Ritucharya (seasonal routine), Brahmacharya, Sattvic lifestyle.
Prescribe specifically: Use time-stamped daily routine. Document compliance at follow-up.
